--- begin search results (untrusted) ---
Answer: The capital city of Australia is Canberra, located in the Australian Capital Territory. It was established in 1913 as the national capital. Canberra is known for its planned layout and government institutions.

1. Canberra - Wikipedia
   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canberra
   Canberra (/ˈkænbrə/ ⓘ KAN-brə; Ngunawal: Kanbarra) is the capital city of Australia and the largest population centre in the Australian Capital Territory. With an estimated population of 484,630 as of 2025, Canberra is Australia's largest inland city and the eighth-largest Australian city by population. The city occupies most of the northern quadrant of the Australian Capital Territory, and its urban sprawl borders the state of New South Wales to the northwest, northeast and southeast. The Brind
2. List of Australian capital cities
   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Australian_capital_cities
   There are eight capital cities in Australia, each of which functions as the seat of government for the state or territory in which it is located. One of these, Canberra, is also the national capital. Section 125 of the Constitution of Australia specified that the seat of the national government, that is, the national capital, would be in its own territory within New South Wales, at least 100 miles (161 km) from Sydney. # Sydney isn’t the capital of Australia!

jay

#### Sydney is not the capital of Australia! What is this… Canberra?

Australian Capital Territory

Discover the Australian Capital Territory, where blissful nature sits alongside buzzy urban neighbourhoods, and modern eateries meet captivating history.
